compiled_state.author: Iso Tester

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
G4 container review (WS1 child vs parent + sibling roster). Findings:

**[G4 / dependency-gap + ordering-violation] WS1's `verifier_cfg` deletion is ordered AFTER WS2 but no dependency edge encodes it, and it contradicts the "WS1 lands FIRST" sequencing.**
- criteria: WS1 AC "Once parity passes, `_run_passes`, `pass2_verify`, and `verifier_cfg` are deleted"; parent Design-decisions "WS1 (migration) lands first ... WS2 → WS3".
- location: WS1 description NOTE ("the deletion of `verifier_cfg` here should land after WS2 so the workflow verify path does not lose the downgrade") and WS1 AC bullet 5; cross-ref WS2 (bc6e) and parent sequencing.
- evidence: (1) WS1 Why + parent both assert WS1 lands FIRST. (2) WS1 NOTE requires the `verifier_cfg` deletion to land AFTER WS2 (so the Sonnet downgrade isn't lost). (3) WS1 deps = []; WS2 deps = []. There is NO `depends_on` edge from WS1→WS2 (or any link) encoding the "verifier_cfg deletion after WS2" constraint — only WS3 declares deps on WS1 and WS2.
- scenarios: WS1 is claimed/landed first per the stated sequencing, deletes `verifier_cfg` before WS2 pins the per-step `model:` Sonnet downgrade on the workflow verify step → the workflow verify path silently reverts to the runner default (Opus), losing the decisive non-frontier verifier downgrade that `verifier_cfg` provided — a behavioral regression the epic explicitly wants to preserve (parent Success Criteria: "applies the Sonnet-by-default verifier model").
- impact: Following the declared dependency graph (WS1 has no dep gating the deletion) lets WS1 ship the `verifier_cfg` deletion before WS2's replacement exists, dropping the Sonnet downgrade. The safeguard lives only as prose in WS1's NOTE, not in an enforceable dep.
- checklist_item: dependency gap; ordering violation
- suggested_fix: Either (a) add an explicit `depends_on` WS2 (bc6e) on WS1 — but that contradicts "WS1 lands first", so more precisely split WS1's deletion step out / gate the `verifier_cfg` deletion specifically on WS2 landing; or (b) reconcile the parent sequencing prose so it states the precise ordering: WS1 migration + `_run_passes`/`pass2_verify` deletion lands first, but the `verifier_cfg` deletion is gated on WS2 pinning the per-step model. The current state (no edge + contradictory "first" vs "after WS2" prose) is ambiguous and risks the downgrade regression.

**[G4 / scope-overlap — return shape vs chunking on workflow_ops.py] benign co-edit, flagged for sequencing awareness only.**
- criteria: WS1 AC "workflow return shape exposes per-finding `decision` + `citations[].path`"; WS3 re-merges verifications by finding `index`.
- location: WS1 file_impact `workflow_ops.py` (return shape) ; WS3 file_impact `workflow_ops.py` (chunk verify inputs).
- evidence: Both stories edit `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/workflow_ops.py`. WS3 already declares depends_on WS1, so the ordering is encoded; WS1's return-shape change (per-finding decision/citations/index granularity) is the contract WS3's index-merge consumes.
- impact: Low — WS3→WS1 dep edge exists, so this is consistent. Noting only that WS1 must land the per-finding `index`/decision granularity that WS3's merge contract relies on; no contradiction.
- checklist_item: scope overlap / consumer impact
- suggested_fix: None required (dep edge already present); ensure WS1's return shape carries the per-finding `index` WS3's merge keys on.

Note: WS4 (coach_notes when-gate) and WS1 both edit plan-review.yaml but in disjoint regions (coach step vs probe-mode/criteria subset) — benign, no finding. Shared-helper retention (pass3_decide etc.) is consistent across WS1 scope and the workflow ops that import them — no residual-reference finding.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
Plan-review PASS (signed, 0 blocking, 10 advisory + 4 overflow). Triage + concrete decisions (remediating in implementation, attestation preserved):
- [E2 fe1efb — pick ONE approach] Probe mode = an optional `probe_criteria` ALLOWLIST input on plan_review_assemble_criteria (when set, include_<ID> is true only for those ids), threaded from a new optional gate-workflow input. NOT a separate drift-probe.yaml — reuse the single gate workflow (no duplicated pipeline).
- [E2 f7f219 / F1 f5c706 — 'granularity' is vague] No NEW return shape needed: the existing plan_review_verdict already partitions blocking[]/advisory[] and each finding carries citations[] with .path. drift_refresh inspects verdict['blocking'] (any → escalate) and the union of blocking+advisory citation paths ∩ drifted-files (→ escalate); else refresh. Specify exactly that.
- [COH f85ed6 / E1 f769a2 — parity-needs-bespoke vs AC5-deletes-bespoke] RESOLVED: WS2 (gawky-koi-grain) already landed + committed the downgrade in the workflow path (_verifier_cfg), so deleting passes.verifier_cfg now does NOT lose the downgrade. Parity is satisfied DURABLY by recording the bespoke probe's escalate/refresh decisions on a small fixture corpus as a GOLDEN before deletion, then asserting the workflow probe reproduces the golden — so the parity test survives deletion (no reference to deleted symbols).
- [E2 fd5b4b — fixture corpus unspecified] Corpus = 3 in-test synthetic PlanContext fixtures: (a) a clean probe (no blocking, no drifted citation) → refresh; (b) a blocking finding → escalate; (c) an advisory finding citing a drifted file → escalate. Lives in tests/unit/test_plan_review.py (or a drift test module).
- [T4 f7ba9e — rollback] The drift-refresh feature is opt-in, default OFF (verify.progressive_drift_refresh); turning it off (the default) fully disables the path — that is the back-out if the workflow probe regresses.
- [T2 f2a2d2 / T5e fe3039 — riskiest slice] Land + evidence the probe-mode workflow producing correct escalate/refresh on the golden fixtures FIRST, then wire drift_refresh to it, then delete bespoke. [P6] AC quality noted.

compiled_state.description:
## Why

The B-RETIRE cutover (commit 68dc9713) made the workflow engine the sole plan-review verdict producer,
but `pass2_verify` / `verifier_cfg` / `_run_passes` are NOT dead — they back the opt-in progressive
drift-refresh probe (`verify.progressive_drift_refresh`, default off → `orchestrator.drift_refresh` →
`_run_passes` → `pass2_verify`). This is the second verify code path that the chunking epic must
eliminate so chunking is a single-site change ("no two divergent batching rules"). Migrate drift-refresh
onto the workflow engine, then delete the bespoke pipeline.

## What

Move the drift-refresh probe's find→verify→decide off the bespoke `_run_passes` and onto the workflow
engine, keeping all the non-LLM orchestration in Python. Delete the now-dead bespoke verify
orchestration only after a verdict-parity gate proves the workflow probe agrees with the bespoke probe.

- Add a "probe mode" to the plan-review workflow: run find→verify→decide over the restricted criteria
  subset `_PROBE_CRITERIA = (E4, G1G2)` instead of the full registry — a `criteria` allowlist input on
  the finder op, or a dedicated `drift-probe.yaml` reusing the same ops/prompts.
- Expose per-finding `decision` + `citations[].path` in the workflow return shape so the escalation
  gate can inspect them against the drifted-file set.
- Rewrite `drift_refresh` (`src/rebar/llm/plan_review/orchestrator.py`) to call the workflow; keep the
  Python orchestration: candidate detection (`attest.drift_refresh_candidate`), dep re-hash, escalation
  on a blocking finding or a finding citing a drifted file, `refresh_attestation`, synthetic PASS verdict.
- Pre-deletion parity gate (expand→contract): run BOTH probes over a fixture corpus and assert the
  escalate-vs-refresh decision agrees; the bespoke path stays behind the (default-off) drift flag as
  the back-out until parity holds.
- Only then delete `_run_passes`, `pass2_verify`, `verifier_cfg`.

## Scope

In scope: probe-mode workflow + return-shape change, `drift_refresh` rewrite, parity test, deletion of
the three bespoke functions. Out of scope: the per-step model key / editor work (WS2), token chunking
(WS3), the coach-gate nit (WS4). Keep shared helpers (`pass3_decide`, `pass3_over_findings`,
`verify_instructions`, `coach_instructions`, `render_coach_notes`, `load_move_registry`) — they are
still used by the workflow ops.

NOTE: WS2 provides the declarative Sonnet verifier downgrade via a per-step `model:` key; the deletion
of `verifier_cfg` here should land after WS2 so the workflow verify path does not lose the downgrade.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `drift_refresh` produces its probe verdict via the workflow engine (find→verify→decide over
      `E4, G1G2`), not `_run_passes`.
- [ ] The drift non-LLM orchestration (candidate detection, dep re-hash, escalation on a blocking
      finding or a finding citing a drifted file, `refresh_attestation`) is preserved.
- [ ] The workflow return shape exposes per-finding `decision` + `citations[].path` at the granularity
      the drift escalation gate requires.
- [ ] A parity test asserts the workflow probe and the bespoke probe reach the same escalate-vs-refresh
      decision over a fixture corpus BEFORE any bespoke code is removed.
- [ ] Once parity passes, `_run_passes`, `pass2_verify`, and `verifier_cfg` are deleted; no dead
      bespoke orchestration remains.
- [ ] The shared helpers remain present and are still imported by the workflow ops.
- [ ] `make lint` + `make check` + the full test suite pass.

file_impact.reason: rewrite drift_refresh to call workflow; delete _run_passes
expose per-finding decision+citations in return shape

compiled_state.title: WS1: Migrate drift-refresh onto the workflow engine + delete bespoke verify pipeline
